page: Amazon Web Services m5zn.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server powered by an Intel Xeon 8252C processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs operating at a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and EBS optimization, supported by a high-speed 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in memory operations and specific compute tasks. The server achieves a top-tier rating in the Passmark memory score and strong results in uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidths, reaching 235.79 GB/sec and 97.89 GB/sec respectively. Single-threaded gzip compression and software compilation with Clang also perform in the top 25%. However, the platform exhibits limitations in LLM text generation, where performance falls into the weak and poor categories for medium and small models.

From a resource density perspective, the m5zn.metal offers high-frequency compute power and high network throughput without the overhead of virtualization. The lack of local storage requires external EBS volumes, which may introduce latency tradeoffs depending on the workload. This server is highly suited for high-performance general-purpose workloads, network-intensive applications, software compilation, and memory-bound database operations, while it is less optimal for local storage-heavy tasks or LLM text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server featuring an Intel Xeon 8252C processor with 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. The server is optimized for high-speed networking with a 100 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory performance and strong software compilation capabilities, though LLM text generation remains a weak point. This hardware profile is highly efficient for compute-heavy, network-intensive, and memory-bound workloads such as software compilation and database operations, offering bare-metal performance without virtualization overhead.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.metal is a bare-metal server featuring a 4.5 GHz Intel Xeon processor, 48 vCPUs, 192 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for high-performance general-purpose workloads.

tagline: A high-frequency bare-metal server delivering rapid memory throughput and 100 Gbps networking for demanding compute and database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8252C processor with 48 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz
192 GB of DDR4 memory with top-tier read/write bandwidth
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput applications
Best suited for software compilation, network-intensive tasks, and databases
